assignment.name: vague
assignment.title: Make something cool with AI
assignment.description: Build a project that uses AI in some interesting way. The exact scope, audience, and form of the deliverable are up to you.

recommended_plan: Analysis or research project (using AI to uncover insights from data)
Public datasets (e.g., Kaggle, government data, social media)
Environmental/climate insights (e.g., carbon emissions patterns, deforestation trends, energy usage)

verdict.notes: The plan is solid and achievable within the timeline—environmental analysis with public datasets is a proven approach with clear deliverables. The main risk is scope creep on the analysis side; recommend committing early to 2-3 specific climate metrics and a tight modeling/visualization scope to ensure quality polish and clear presentation of insights.

root.chosen_best_option: Analysis or research project (using AI to uncover insights from data)

branches.option: Interactive tool/application (web app, chatbot, or API that users engage with)

history: Interactive tool/application (web app, chatbot, or API that users engage with)

verdict.notes: The plan is feasible and targets an engaging application, but lacks specificity on scope, dataset source, and technical stack—which creates execution risk. Recommend immediately deciding on platform (web vs. standalone), concrete data source (public API like Twitter, Reddit, or provided dataset), and which pre-trained model to use (e.g., Hugging Face transformers) to avoid scope creep in the final week.

Generative AI (text/image generation, creative content)

Multimodal projects are ambitious for 14 days—clarify whether you're building an end-to-end feature or integrating existing APIs (the latter is more realistic). verdict.notes: The plan is feasible and has genuine appeal—conversational AI with memory/personality for creative collaboration is intrinsically interesting and achievable in 14 days. However, it lacks concrete technical specifics: no stack chosen, no clarity on persistence/memory implementation, no defined scope boundaries (e.g., web vs. CLI, which LLM provider, what 'personality' means functionally), and no MVP definition—the vagueness risks scope creep or a shallow final product. Adding one decision layer (e.g., 'Next.js + OpenAI API + session-based memory + character-focused roleplay for writers') would significantly improve execution confidence.

verdict.notes: The plan is technically feasible within the deadline and hits the 'interesting' + 'AI' requirements, but lacks specificity on differentiation and implementation details. Mental health/counseling domains carry ethical/liability risks that may complicate deployment—technical debugging or career counseling is safer. Success depends heavily on clear scoping of MVP features (conversation length, personalization depth, error handling) and whether existing APIs (OpenAI, Hugging Face) will be used to compress development time.

verdict.notes: The plan lacks specificity and tries to do too much for 14 days—fine-tuning a model on a custom dataset alone typically requires 5-7 days (data prep, infrastructure setup, training, validation). branches.option: Personal/collected data (e.g., your own logs, surveys, scraping)

history: Analysis or research project (using AI to uncover insights from data)
Personal/collected data (e.g., your own logs, surveys, scraping)

verdict.notes: The plan is feasible and has strong novelty potential—analyzing personal behavioral data for patterns is inherently interesting and relatable. verdict.notes: The plan is sensible and achievable within 14 days—sentiment/trend analysis on scraped data is a proven template.
